keyTerms.Runnymede: The Thames-side meadow where King John sealed Magna Carta on June 15, 1215, chosen as neutral ground between royalist Windsor and rebel-held London
keyTerms.due process: The legal principle that the state must respect all legal rights owed to a person, directly descended from Magna Carta's Clause 39
keyTerms.baronial grievances: The specific complaints of England's feudal aristocracy against King John, including excessive inheritance taxes and forced widow remarriages
keyTerms.Henry III: John's nine-year-old son who became king in 1216; his regency government reissued Magna Carta in modified form, embedding it into English law
keyTerms.statute rolls: The official parchment records of Acts of Parliament, onto which Magna Carta was formally entered in 1297 under Edward I
